reasoning: All four nail the central point that a 19/20 win rate is the expected signature of a negative-skew short-vol payoff, not evidence of edge. A wins by combining the explicit question-substitution refusal, a binomial sanity check (5% per-quarter tail implies a clean 20q run is unremarkable), a named reference class, a clean trap taxonomy (resulting, hindsight, anchoring on 3x), the gamma/liquidity non-linearity, and the most actionable staged verdict (1.25-1.5x with a survival-tested re-entry condition). D is nearly equal with the strongest bias work (turkey problem, survivorship of vanished desks, skin-in-the-game) and a sharp ruin constraint, edged out only by A's quantification and requirement structure. C is strong and original (loss asymmetry, which-5-years, mismarking of illiquid options, desk-to-firm decision-owner shift) but lighter on the cognitive-bias layer; B is rigorous on the quant package (ES at 97.5/99/99.5, loss-to-premium math, model validation of jumps) but flattest on bias surfacing and non-linearity, so it ranks last.
